page: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture, delivering a clock speed of 4.5 GHz across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512_vnni and AVX512_bf16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance includes 945 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, but does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average middle 50% category. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only small block cached and large block uncached transfers, also register average performance. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speed at 403.97 tokens per second and strong prompt processing speed. However, performance drops to average for medium 7B models, and is weak for large 70B models, reflecting the lack of dedicated hardware acceleration for large-scale AI workloads.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high memory capacity relative to compute cores, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The inclusion of 945 GB of local storage adds value for applications requiring fast local scratch space. Tradeoffs include average multi-threaded scaling and a lack of GPU acceleration, which limits its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale deep learning. This server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data processing pipelines, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 x86_64 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It includes 945 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it lacks GPU resources. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, with single-threaded compression and decompression placing in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. The server demonstrates strong to top-tier inference speeds on small language models, but performance degrades on larger models. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, data processing, and lightweight text generation workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, suited for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity RAM, and integrated local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM providing a 1:8 memory-optimized ratio
AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 4.5 GHz clock speed and AVX-512 support
945 GB of bundled local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and small-scale LLM inference
